body:
## Finding

**Source**: AUDIT_RENDERER_2026-04-22 · Dimension 6 — Shader Correctness
**File**: `crates/renderer/shaders/triangle.frag:174-176`, `crates/renderer/src/vertex.rs`
**Severity**: MEDIUM

The global vertex SSBO is declared as a flat `float[]`:

```glsl
layout(std430, set = 1, binding = 8) readonly buffer GlobalVertices {
    float vertexData[]; // flat array, stride = 21 floats (84 bytes)
};
```

The Rust `Vertex` struct layout is:
- bytes 0–11: position (f32×3) ✓ safe as float
- bytes 12–23: color (f32×3) ✓ safe as float
- bytes 24–35: normal (f32×3) ✓ safe as float
- bytes 36–43: uv (f32×2) ✓ safe as float — **only these are read today**
- bytes 44–59: bone_indices (**u32×4** — NOT floats, reinterpretation hazard)
- bytes 60–75: bone_weights (f32×4) ✓ safe as float
- bytes 68–75: splat_0/1 (**u8×8 unpacked** — NOT floats)

Today the shader only reads UV at float indices 9-10 (bytes 36-43), which are genuine f32s. However, any future RT hit shader reading bone_indices (`float vertexData[base + 11..14]`) or splat bytes (`float vertexData[base + 17..18]`) would silently reinterpret u32/u8 bit patterns as f32.

This creates a pit-of-failure for RT shader authors who follow the existing pattern of `vertexData[base + N]` access.

## Fix

Short-term: Add a comment at triangle.frag:175:
```glsl
// WARNING: Only offsets 0-10 (position+color+normal+uv = 44 bytes) are safely
// readable as float. bone_indices (offsets 11-14) are u32, splat weights
// (offsets 17-18) are u8. Do NOT read those as float in RT hit shaders.
float vertexData[];
```

Long-term: Add a separate `uvData[]` SSBO (layout: `vec2 uv[]`, stride 8 bytes) for clean RT hit UV lookups, eliminating the reinterpretation concern entirely.

## Completeness Checks
- [ ] **SIBLING**: Check all RT hit sites in triangle.frag that read from `vertexData[]` — confirm none access bone_indices/splat offsets
- [ ] **SIBLING**: Check caustic_splat.comp — if it reads from the global vertex SSBO, same audit needed
- [ ] **TESTS**: Static analysis / grep for `vertexData\[.*\+ 1[1-9]\]` patterns across all shaders
title: SH-1: triangle.frag reads vertex SSBO as float[] — bone/splat fields are not floats (RT hit reinterpretation risk)
